questions.question: Explain the working principle of a 4-to-1 multiplexer with the help of its truth table.
keyPoints: A 4-to-1 MUX has 4 data inputs (I0-I3), 2 select lines (S1, S0), and 1 output (Y)
When S1S0=00, output = I0; when 01, output = I1; when 10, output = I2; when 11, output = I3
The select lines act as binary address to select which input passes through

questions.question: Draw the logic circuit diagram of a 2-to-1 multiplexer and derive its Boolean expression.
keyPoints: 2-to-1 MUX has inputs I0, I1, select line S, and output Y
Logic uses two AND gates and one OR gate
Boolean expression: Y = I0.S' + I1.S
When S=0, Y=I0; when S=1, Y=I1

questions.question: How can a multiplexer be used to implement a Boolean function? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Connect function variables to select lines
Apply constants (0,1) or remaining variables to data inputs
For n-variable function using 2^n:1 MUX, use n-1 variables on select lines
The remaining variable and constants determine data input values

questions.question: Design a 8-to-1 multiplexer using two 4-to-1 multiplexers and one 2-to-1 multiplexer.
keyPoints: Use two 4-to-1 MUX for first stage handling 8 inputs
Connect select lines S2S1 to first stage MUXes
Use S0 and 2-to-1 MUX to select between outputs of two 4-to-1 MUXes
OR gate can also be used for final stage combining

questions.question: What is the difference between a multiplexer and a demultiplexer?
keyPoints: MUX: Many inputs to ONE output (data selector)
DEMUX: ONE input to Many outputs (data distributor)
Both use select lines for routing
MUX selects which input reaches output; DEMUX selects which output receives input

questions.question: Explain the concept of enable input in a multiplexer. Why is it important?
keyPoints: Enable (EN) input controls MUX activation
When EN=1 (active), MUX operates normally
When EN=0 (inactive), output disabled (0 or high-impedance)
Allows cascading multiple MUXes and controls operation timing

questions.question: List and explain at least four applications of multiplexers in digital systems.
keyPoints: Data Selection: Choose between multiple data sources
Parallel-to-Serial Conversion: Route parallel bits one at a time
Logic Function Generation: Implement Boolean functions
Route Control: Direct data flow in communication systems
Memory Selection: Select different memory banks in microprocessor systems

questions.question: Design a 4-to-1 multiplexer using AND, OR, and NOT gates. Also derive its Boolean expression and explain how the selection works for all possible combinations of select lines.
keyPoints: Draw circuit with 4 AND gates, 1 OR gate, 2 NOT gates
Boolean expression: Y = I0.S1'.S0' + I1.S1'.S0 + I2.S1.S0' + I3.S1.S0
For S1S0=00: AND1 enabled, output=I0; for 01: AND2, output=I1
For S1S0=10: AND3 enabled, output=I2; for 11: AND4, output=I3
Each AND gate responds to unique select line combination

questions.question: Implement the Boolean function F(A,B,C) = Σm(1,3,5,6) using a 8-to-1 multiplexer.
keyPoints: Function has 3 variables: A, B, C
For 8-to-1 MUX, connect A, B to select lines S1, S0
C and constants go to data inputs
For minterms where F=1: I1= C (m1=001, m3=011 need C), I3= C' (m3 when C=1? wait)
Truth table analysis shows which inputs need C, C', 0, or 1

questions.question: Explain how to construct a 16-to-1 multiplexer using multiple 4-to-1 multiplexers. Draw the block diagram and explain the selection process.
keyPoints: Need 5 units of 4-to-1 MUX for 16 inputs
First stage: 4 MUX handle 16 inputs (4 each)
Second stage: 1 MUX combines outputs of first stage
Use 2 select lines for each first-stage MUX
Use 2 select lines at second stage for final selection
Total select lines: 4 bits (2^4 = 16)

questions.question: Design a 2-to-1 multiplexer using (a) basic gates (AND, OR, NOT), (b) only NAND gates, and (c) only NOR gates. Compare the three implementations.
keyPoints: Basic gates: Y = I0.S' + I1.S using 2 AND, 1 OR, 1 NOT
NAND implementation: Use De Morgan's theorem to convert
NOR implementation: Similar transformation required
All implementations produce same truth table
Difference in propagation delay and gate count
